text: "Hello?" "Yes." "Good evening. My name is Jacob. So I'm calling because there are pending grocery and cash benefits worth up to $300 available on your Medicare account through Connecticut State Relief Program. So have you been notified about these benefits yet?" "No, I haven't." "Oh, that's all right. That's exactly why I'm calling. And I want to help you get these benefits processed before they go unclaimed. You're covered under Part A and Part B, yes?" "Yes." "Perfect. You qualify for the benefits." "I'm forwarding you to our supervisor. Please hold on." "Okay." "Thank you so much for holding this suggestion with Medicare benefits. How are you doing today?" "Hi." "Good to hear that. So how old are you? What's your age?" "My age, 80 years old and three months." "No." "Okay, and are you getting any kind of health insurance right now? Through your previous employment, any union or retirement, VA or dry care for life?" "Yes." "No." "Any kind of secondary insurance you have, like Humana, United Health Care, ECMA, or any kind that you have?" "No."
